question: Based on the stacked bar chart showing quarterly restaurant performance indicators, which statement is most appropriate?

options.A: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it combines unrelated metrics that cannot be meaningfully added together.
options.B: Q1 clearly has the best overall performance because it has the lowest values for complaints and temperature.
options.C: Q3 clearly has the best overall performance because it has the highest stacked total.
options.D: Q4 clearly has the best overall performance because its indicators appear most balanced.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Sales, Kitchen Temperature, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
